open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at viewer.js loadDocument() (the single viewer load boundary), and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's three new modules add NO new unbounded surface: attachments.js (TASK-360) IS a new ingest boundary but the MILDEST of the family — it reads ONE fresh user-picked file of ANY type and embeds its raw bytes (OPAQUE, never parsed/decoded -> no PDF-bomb risk on the attachment) into a pdf-lib copy of the open doc; single-file-per-action (no queue) with a dedicated 25 MB cap + empty guard, so peak memory = getData() + <=25 MB + output, bounded -> needs NO cumulative-bytes cap. Its extract path sanitizes embedded filenames (safeAttachmentName: strips path separators + non-[a-zA-Z0-9._-], slice 180 -> no path traversal) and downloads as application/octet-stream (forced download, hostile bytes never rendered). font-inspector.js (TASK-361) and links.js (TASK-363) are read-only inspectors: they touch ONLY the already-validated open doc (getData()/pdf.js getAnnotations()), upload/download/mutate nothing -> NOT ingest boundaries; both walk all pages (SEC-002 per-page consumer family, bounded by the single viewer-load cap, low memory). Same LOW self-DoS family overall; all bounded by the same single fix: page-count cap once at viewer load boundary + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles(). Developer task, not security's to implement.
open_gaps.SEC-CSP-FRAME: INFO. frame-src 'self' blob: in the live CSP (added 2026-06-13 for the Print tool, print.js). Allows only same-origin + same-origin-blob frames, no remote origins; frame-ancestors 'self' unchanged. Verified live this tick.

notes: 53 JS modules (+3 since the 2026-06-13 20:25 split-chunks/flatten review: attachments.js TASK-360, font-inspector.js TASK-361, links.js TASK-363). ALL THREE CLEAN, no new findings. attachments.js is the lone new ingest boundary and the mildest of the family (opaque single-file embed, dedicated 25 MB cap, no cumulative queue, no decode -> no bomb risk; extract path sanitizes filenames -> no path traversal, octet-stream forced download). font-inspector.js + links.js are read-only inspectors operating only on the already-validated open viewer doc (getData() / pdf.js getAnnotations()) -> not ingest boundaries, nothing uploaded/downloaded/mutated. links.js is XSS-hardened: link URLs (incl unsafeUrl) rendered as plain textContent, NEVER a clickable <a href>, so javascript:/data: URIs can neither execute nor be navigated. font-inspector caps Form XObject recursion at depth 1 with a visited ref-set cycle guard. New-file audit: zero real innerHTML/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML/document.write/eval/new Function/fetch/XHR/<a href> sinks across all 3 (only innerHTML='' clears + the one safe textContent line + descriptive comments); node --check clean; repo web/ vs /var/www webroot diff CLEAN for links.js/font-inspector.js/attachments.js/app.js/index.html. NB: TASK-362 permissions.js is still TODO (not yet built; not in webroot) — nothing to review there this tick. Live headers via curl: full resource CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; worker-src 'self' blob:; img-src 'self' blob: data:; connect-src 'self' blob:; frame-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; font-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. nginx -t OK. Path blocking: .git/config 403, CLAUDE.md 403, *.sh 403, js/*.js 200. SSH: 0 failed/invalid auth in last 24h.
